concerns.text: SC-001 states 'explaining >15% of variance' but defers empirical values. The threshold '15%' is a specific empirical claim, not a metric definition. It must be changed to '[deferred]' or a reference to a community standard to be measurable without pre-asserting results.

FR-006 requires reporting 'variation in false-positive/false-negative rates' but the spec does not define the ground truth labels required to calculate these rates. Without a defined ground truth (e.g., known active interactions), FPR/FNR are unverifiable.

FR-008 mandates 'multiple-comparison correction (e.g., Bonferroni or FDR)' but does not specify which method to use or the alpha level. 'e.g.' makes the requirement non-deterministic; the implementer cannot know which test to run to pass.

US-3 Acceptance Scenario 2 requires outputting 'false-positive and false-negative rates' but, as with FR-006, no ground truth is defined in the spec to calculate these. The test procedure is impossible to execute as written.
